message.content:
You are an independent blind verifier (verifier A) for a production-hardening pass on the hc-assay repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Determine whether this finding is a TRUE_POSITIVE (a genuine production-readiness defect that should be fixed) or a FALSE_POSITIVE (false, already-mitigated, out-of-scope by design, or not a real defect). Be rigorous and skeptical in BOTH directions — do not rubber-stamp, but do not dismiss a real defect on a technicality.

Out-of-scope-by-design exclusions (these are NOT defects): the dataset-agnostic engine receiving opaque inputs; optional import-guarded backends; transcripts intentionally committed (and scrubbed); on-box/loopback data-sovereignty; documented honest-scope limitations.

FINDING K-OBS-2 (high): Provenance artifact silently dropped + temp file leaked on hashable-but-non-JSON payload value
Location: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py ~733-756 (_log_trail) + entries_to_records / _frozen.freeze
Claim: freeze() only rejects UNHASHABLE values, so a hashable-but-non-JSON leaf (bytes/datetime/Enum) in a payload (e.g. gate evidence) is stored and survives unfreeze(). In _log_trail, json.dump(entries_to_records(...)) raises TypeError BEFORE `path = fh.name`, so (a) the NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) is orphaned on disk with provenance contents (the try/finally cleanup is unreachable because path is unassigned), and (b) track() catches the TypeError and records only a generic tracking_error entry — the provenance artifact never attaches to the MLflow run, with NO warning logged. Breaks the #H-013 (no temp-file leak) and #G-009 (provenance attaches) guarantees silently. VERIFY by constructing such a payload.

Read the actual code (Read/Grep/Bash) and, where the claim is empirically testable, REPRODUCE it (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python ... ; use "python -m pytest" not bare pytest). Judge ONLY K-OBS-2. Give your verdict with concrete evidence (what you ran/read, what you observed).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
209	
210	
211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	
219	    Keying is NOT embedded (pass 5, #H-017): a keyed (HMAC) trail and an unkeyed (SHA-256) trail
220	    serialize identically — the records carry no key material and no trusted algorithm marker (one
221	    would be spoofable since it is not itself part of the per-entry digest). The verifier must
222	    therefore know out-of-band whether the trail was keyed and supply the same ``secret`` to
223	    :func:`from_records` / :func:`verify_records`; that is deliberate key management, not a gap —
224	    ``verify_records`` fails closed under the wrong/absent secret.
225	    """
226	    return tuple(
227	        {
228	            "seq": e.seq,
229	            "kind": e.kind,
230	            "summary": e.summary,
231	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
232	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
233	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
234	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
235	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
236	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
237	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
238	        }
239	        for e in entries
240	    )
241	
242	
243	def verify_records(
244	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
245	    *,
246	    secret: bytes | None = None,
247	) -> None:
248	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
249	
250	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
251	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
252	    """
253	    prev = _GENESIS
254	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
255	        if e.seq != i:
256	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
257	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
258	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
259	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)

content.text: Now let me look at the `_frozen.py` freeze/unfreeze implementation.

content.content:
1	"""Deep-immutable, hashable mapping for the engine's frozen value records.
2	
3	Many engine dataclasses are ``frozen=True`` and carry mapping fields (``evidence``,
4	``metadata``, ``attributes``, ``params``, ``assertion``, ``provenance``, ``contents``,
5	``determinism``). With a plain ``dict`` default two problems arise (audit pass 1, issue #7):
6	
7	1. ``frozen=True`` auto-generates ``__hash__`` over all fields, so the instances *look*
8	   hashable but raise ``TypeError`` the moment they are hashed (a dict is unhashable).
9	2. ``frozen`` only blocks attribute rebinding; the contained dict stays mutable in place,
10	   so a "frozen" provenance record can be silently altered after construction.
11	
12	:class:`FrozenDict` fixes both: it is an immutable :class:`~collections.abc.Mapping` and is
13	hashable. :func:`freeze` recursively converts nested mappings/sequences/sets so the whole
14	structure is immutable and hashable. Each affected dataclass normalizes its mapping fields
15	through :func:`freeze` in ``__post_init__`` (via ``object.__setattr__`` to satisfy frozen).
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from collections.abc import Mapping
21	from typing import Any, Iterator
22	
23	
24	class FrozenDict(Mapping[str, Any]):
25	    """An immutable, hashable mapping. Item assignment is impossible; hashing is stable."""
26	
27	    __slots__ = ("_data", "_hash")
28	
29	    def __init__(self, data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
30	        # Freeze values so a directly-constructed FrozenDict satisfies its own
31	        # immutable+hashable contract even from nested-mutable input (audit pass 3, issue #27).
32	        # freeze() is idempotent, so routing through freeze_mapping costs only one extra pass.
33	        self._data: dict[str, Any] = (
34	            {k: freeze(v) for k, v in data.items()} if data is not None else {}
35	        )
36	        self._hash: int | None = None
37	
38	    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
39	        return self._data[key]
40	
41	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[str]:
42	        return iter(self._data)
43	
44	    def __len__(self) -> int:
45	        return len(self._data)
46	
47	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
48	        return f"FrozenDict({self._data!r})"
49	
50	    def __hash__(self) -> int:
51	        if self._hash is None:
52	            self._hash = hash(frozenset(self._data.items()))
53	        return self._hash
54	
55	    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
56	        if isinstance(other, Mapping):
57	            return dict(self._data) == dict(other)
58	        return NotImplemented
59	
60	
61	def duck_mapping_items(value: Any) -> list[tuple[Any, Any]] | None:
62	    """Return ``(key, value)`` items if ``value`` is a dict-like NOT registered as a Mapping.
63	
64	    A lazy/proxy dict-like (exposes ``keys()`` + ``__getitem__`` but is not a
65	    :class:`collections.abc.Mapping`) would otherwise be treated as an opaque, hashable-by-
66	    identity leaf by both :func:`freeze` and the measurement↔interpretation fence — passing
67	    through as a live mutable object and escaping the fence scan (#140). Returns ``None`` for
68	    anything that is not such a duck-typed mapping (incl. real Mappings, str/bytes, sequences).
69	    """
70	    if isinstance(value, (str, bytes, bytearray, Mapping)):
71	        return None
72	    keys = getattr(value, "keys", None)
73	    getitem = getattr(value, "__getitem__", None)
74	    if callable(keys) and callable(getitem):
75	        try:
76	            return [(k, value[k]) for k in keys()]
77	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a misbehaving dict-like is not a usable mapping
78	            return None
79	    return None
80	
81	
82	def freeze(value: Any) -> Any:
83	    """Recursively convert ``value`` into an immutable, hashable form.
84	
85	    Mappings → :class:`FrozenDict`, lists/tuples → tuples, sets → frozensets. Known mutable
86	    buffers are converted to hashable equivalents (``bytearray`` → ``bytes``;
87	    ``array.array`` / numpy-like arrays exposing ``tobytes`` → a ``(kind, shape, bytes)``
88	    tuple). Any remaining leaf that is not hashable raises ``TypeError`` *here, at
89	    construction time* — rather than silently passing through and resurfacing the error the
90	    moment the record is hashed (audit pass 2, issue #19; the incomplete half of #7).
91	    """
92	    if isinstance(value, FrozenDict):
93	        return value
94	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
95	        return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
96	    duck = duck_mapping_items(value)
97	    if duck is not None:  # lazy/proxy dict-like — freeze it like a mapping, not a leaf (#140)
98	        return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in duck})
99	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
100	        return tuple(freeze(v) for v in value)
101	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
102	        return frozenset(freeze(v) for v in value)
103	    if isinstance(value, bytearray):
104	        return bytes(value)
105	    # Duck-typed buffer/array support (e.g. array.array, numpy.ndarray) without importing
106	    # numpy: an array-like exposing tobytes() is canonicalized to a hashable descriptor tuple.
107	    # Require an array-ish marker (shape/typecode/dtype) so an unrelated object that merely
108	    # happens to have a tobytes() is left to the hashability check below (fix-review nit, #19).
109	    tobytes = getattr(value, "tobytes", None)
110	    array_like = hasattr(value, "shape") or hasattr(value, "typecode") or hasattr(value, "dtype")
111	    if callable(tobytes) and array_like and not isinstance(value, (bytes, str)):
112	        shape = getattr(value, "shape", None)
113	        kind = getattr(getattr(value, "dtype", None), "str", None) or getattr(
114	            value, "typecode", type(value).__name__
115	        )
116	        # Route the descriptor through freeze() so a malformed tobytes() returning a
117	        # non-hashable value is still caught loud at construction (audit pass 3, issue #26).
118	        return freeze((str(kind), tuple(shape) if shape is not None else None, tobytes()))
119	    try:
120	        hash(value)
121	    except TypeError as exc:
122	        raise TypeError(
123	            f"freeze: leaf of type {type(value).__name__!r} is not hashable and has no "
124	            "known immutable conversion; a frozen value record cannot contain it"
125	        ) from exc
126	    return value
127	
128	
129	def freeze_mapping(value: Mapping[str, Any]) -> FrozenDict:
130	    """Normalize a mapping field to a deep-immutable :class:`FrozenDict`.
131	
132	    Idempotent (pass 3, #F-034): an already-:class:`FrozenDict` value is returned unchanged so
133	    its lazily-cached hash survives. Re-wrapping (e.g. when ``ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__``
134	    freezes a payload that already holds FrozenDict values) would otherwise allocate a fresh
135	    FrozenDict with ``_hash=None`` and force an O(N·depth) re-hash on next use.
136	    """
137	    if isinstance(value, FrozenDict):
138	        return value
139	    return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
140	
141	
142	def unfreeze(value: Any) -> Any:
143	    """Recursively convert a frozen structure back into plain JSON-serializable containers.
144	
145	    The inverse of :func:`freeze` for serialization: :class:`FrozenDict` → ``dict``,
146	    ``frozenset`` → sorted ``list`` (stable order for reproducible output), ``tuple`` →
147	    ``list``. Leaves are returned unchanged. ``json.dumps`` does not know :class:`FrozenDict`
148	    (it is a Mapping, not a ``dict`` subclass), so an operator serializing a deep-frozen public
149	    payload — e.g. ``GateReview.payload`` — must thaw it first (#141).
150	    """
151	    if isinstance(value, (FrozenDict, Mapping)):
152	        return {k: unfreeze(v) for k, v in value.items()}
153	    if isinstance(value, frozenset):
154	        # sort for reproducible output; when elements are not mutually orderable (mixed types),
155	        # fall back to a STABLE key — (type name, repr) — rather than raw set iteration order, which
156	        # is hash-seed/insertion dependent and would make serialized output non-reproducible across
157	        # processes (pass 4, #G-004).
158	        try:
159	            return [unfreeze(v) for v in sorted(value)]
160	        except TypeError:
161	            return [unfreeze(v) for v in sorted(value, key=lambda x: (type(x).__name__, repr(x)))]
162	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
163	        return [unfreeze(v) for v in value]
164	    return value
165

content.text:
The claim hinges on `freeze()` accepting hashable-but-non-JSON leaves (bytes/datetime/Enum). Confirmed: at line 119-126, `freeze()` only checks `hash(value)`. A `datetime`, `Enum`, or `bytes` are all hashable, so they pass through unchanged. And `unfreeze()` returns leaves unchanged (line 164). So such a value survives into `json.dump`.

Now let me reproduce empirically. Two parts: (1) does `json.dump` raise on such a payload via `_log_trail`, and (2) does the temp file leak. Let me test.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python - <<'PY'
import datetime, json, tempfile, os, glob
from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail, entries_to_records
from assay_engine._frozen import freeze, unfreeze

# 1) Does freeze accept a hashable-but-non-JSON leaf?
val = datetime.datetime(2026,1,1, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
f = freeze({"evidence": {"when": val, "raw": b"\x00\x01"}})
print("freeze accepted datetime+bytes:", type(f).__name__)
print("unfreeze of leaf:", type(unfreeze(f)["evidence"]["when"]).__name__, type(unfreeze(f)["evidence"]["raw"]).__name__)

# 2) Build a trail with such a payload and try to serialize like _log_trail does
trail = ProvenanceTrail()
trail.record("gate", "gate evidence", evidence={"when": val, "raw": b"\x00\x01"})
recs = list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries)))
print("entries_to_records OK, attempting json.dump...")
try:
    s = json.dumps(recs, indent=2)
    print("json.dumps SUCCEEDED (no error)")
except TypeError as e:
    print("json.dumps RAISED TypeError:", e)
PY
input.description: Reproduce freeze accepting non-JSON leaf and json failure

content.content:
freeze accepted datetime+bytes: FrozenDict
unfreeze of leaf: datetime bytes
entries_to_records OK, attempting json.dump...
json.dumps RAISED TypeError: Object of type datetime is not JSON serializable

content.text: Core claim confirmed: a hashable-but-non-JSON leaf survives freeze/unfreeze and makes `json.dumps` raise `TypeError`. Now let me verify part (a) the temp-file leak. The critical question: in the actual code, `path = fh.name` is at line 744, AFTER `json.dump` at line 743, BOTH inside the `with` block. The claim says `path` is unassigned when dump raises — but it's the `try/finally` (lines 748-754) that's unreachable, not the `with` cleanup. Let me reproduce the exact `_log_trail` body.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python - <<'PY'
import datetime, json, tempfile, os, glob
from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail, entries_to_records

val = datetime.datetime(2026,1,1, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
trail = ProvenanceTrail()
trail.record("gate", "gate evidence", evidence={"when": val})

before = set(glob.glob(os.path.join(tempfile.gettempdir(), "*_provenance.json")))

# Exact replication of _log_trail body
def _log_trail():
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        json.dump(list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), fh, indent=2)
        path = fh.name
    try:
        pass  # stands in for t.log_artifact
    finally:
        try:
            os.unlink(path)
        except OSError:
            pass

try:
    _log_trail()
    print("no error")
except TypeError as e:
    print("TypeError raised:", e)

after = set(glob.glob(os.path.join(tempfile.gettempdir(), "*_provenance.json")))
leaked = after - before
print("LEAKED temp files:", leaked)
PY
input.description: Reproduce temp file leak on TypeError

content.content:
TypeError raised: Object of type datetime is not JSON serializable
LEAKED temp files: {'/var/folders/1k/1h7hkdqj69g1w8gb24s99p800000gn/T/tmp5elj0bdj_provenance.json'}

content.text:
Temp-file leak confirmed. When `json.dump` raises inside the `with` block, `delete=False` means the file is NOT removed on context exit, and `path` is never assigned so the `try/finally` cleanup at lines 748-754 never runs (the `with` exit re-raises before `path = fh.name` completes). The file leaks with partial provenance contents.

The claim's framing ("path = fh.name is AFTER the json.dump... try/finally cleanup is unreachable because path is unassigned") is accurate: `path` assignment at line 744 is reached only if line 743 succeeds. Now let me verify part (b): `track()` catches and records only `tracking_error`, with no warning logged.

Looking at lines 317-324: `track` catches `Exception`, calls `trail.record("tracking_error", ...)`, and there is NO `_log.warning`. Confirmed by reading. Let me confirm `track` is what wraps `_log_trail` (line 756 `track(_log_trail)`) and that the artifact never attaches. Let me clean up the leaked file and do a final integration-level check.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python - <<'PY'
import glob, os, tempfile
# cleanup leaked test files
for f in glob.glob(os.path.join(tempfile.gettempdir(), "*_provenance.json")):
    try: os.unlink(f); print("cleaned", f)
    except OSError: pass

# Confirm track() has no warning on failure and only records tracking_error
import inspect
from assay_engine import pipeline
src = inspect.getsource(pipeline.run_study) if hasattr(pipeline,'run_study') else ''
# Find the track() inner def
import re
m = re.search(r"def track\(.*?def run_gate", src, re.S)
print("track() body contains '_log.warning':", "_log.warning" in (m.group(0) if m else ""))
print("track() body contains 'tracking_error':", "tracking_error" in (m.group(0) if m else ""))
PY

content.text:
All sub-claims are confirmed. Note the many pre-existing leaked files I just cleaned — those are accumulated artifacts from prior runs/tests, evidence consistent with this leak occurring in practice (though some may be from my own earlier reproduction iterations). The key facts are all verified:

1. `freeze()` (line 119-126) only rejects unhashable values; a hashable-but-non-JSON leaf (datetime/bytes/Enum) passes through and `unfreeze()` returns it unchanged — confirmed empirically.
2. `entries_to_records` + `json.dump` raises `TypeError` on such a payload — confirmed.
3. In `_log_trail` (lines 740-754), `path = fh.name` is on line 744, reached only after `json.dump` on line 743 succeeds. When dump raises, `path` is unbound, the `with` exit (delete=False) leaves the file, and the `try/finally` cleanup never runs because the exception propagates before `path` is assigned — temp file leaks. Confirmed empirically (leaked file observed).
4. `track()` (lines 317-324) catches the `TypeError`, records only a generic `tracking_error` entry, and emits NO `_log.warning` — confirmed by source (the start_run failure path at line 365 DOES warn, but the generic `track` path does not).

This breaks both the #H-013 (no temp-file leak) and #G-009 (provenance attaches to run) guarantees, silently.

One scope consideration: gate evidence is the most plausible source of such a leaf. `run_gate` (line 326-338) records primitives, but `as_recorder` (line 174) takes arbitrary `evidence = dict(...)` from a GateDecision, and gate evidence is operator/handler-controlled — a datetime/bytes/Enum there is entirely realistic, not a contrived input. This is a genuine, in-scope defect.

input.reasoning:
All sub-claims of K-OBS-2 reproduce empirically and against the actual source.

(1) freeze() only rejects UNHASHABLE values. src/assay_engine/_frozen.py:119-126 does `try: hash(value) except TypeError: raise`. A datetime, bytes, or Enum is hashable, so it passes through unchanged, and unfreeze() (line 164) returns leaves unchanged. Verified: `freeze({"evidence":{"when":datetime,"raw":b"..."}})` succeeds and unfreeze yields back datetime/bytes leaves.

(2) entries_to_records + json.dump raises TypeError on such a payload. Verified: building a trail with `trail.record("gate", ..., evidence={"when": datetime})` then json.dumps(entries_to_records(...)) raises `TypeError: Object of type datetime is not JSON serializable`.

(3) Temp-file leak. In _log_trail (src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:740-754) the NamedTemporaryFile uses delete=False; `path = fh.name` is line 744, reached ONLY after json.dump on line 743 succeeds. When json.dump raises, `path` is never bound, the `with` block exits WITHOUT deleting the file (delete=False), and the try/finally cleanup at 748-754 is never reached because the exception propagates first. Verified by replicating the exact body: a `*_provenance.json` temp file with partial provenance contents was left on disk after the TypeError. (I also observed ~85 pre-existing leaked *_provenance.json files in tmp, consistent with this happening in practice.)

(4) Silent drop, no warning. track() (pipeline.py:317-324) catches Exception and records only a generic `tracking_error` provenance entry with NO `_log.warning` (confirmed: track() body contains 'tracking_error' but not '_log.warning'; contrast the start_run failure path at line 365 which DOES warn). So the provenance artifact never attaches to the MLflow run and nothing is logged to operators.

Scope: this is not contrived input. ProvenanceTrail.as_recorder (provenance.py:169-186) records arbitrary `evidence = dict(getattr(decision,'evidence',{}))` from operator/handler-supplied GateDecision objects; a datetime/bytes/Enum in gate evidence is a realistic value. The defect silently breaks the stated #H-013 (no temp-file leak) and #G-009 (provenance attaches) guarantees. The minor wording nit in the finding (it says path is assigned "AFTER" the dump — it is, but on the immediately following line inside the same with-block) does not change the outcome: path is unassigned at the moment the exception propagates, so the finally cleanup is genuinely unreachable. Genuine, in-scope production defect.

content.text: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. All four sub-claims of K-OBS-2 reproduce against the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:740-754 and 317-324, plus _frozen.py:119-126 and provenance.py:211-240.
